bc.title: Big Catch (Weight Distro) Analyzer
bc.explain_0: With 0 points, your catches will naturally span the entire database range. You have no artificial floor or ceiling limits applied.
bc.explain_pos_2: points, your absolute worst-case RNG roll (0.0) is mathematically forced up to the
bc.explain_pos_3: % percentile. This guarantees you will NEVER catch a fish smaller than
bc.explain_neg_2: points, your absolute best-case RNG roll (1.0) is mathematically capped at the
bc.explain_neg_3: % percentile. This means it is physically impossible to catch a fish larger than

luck.title: Luck & Drop Rate Analyzer
luck.yields_desc: This table shows the estimated number of fish you will catch of each rarity over a set number of total catches based on your current luck. Decimals indicate fractional chances (e.g., 0.50 means you have a 50% chance to see at least 1).

matrix.search_placeholder: Search by fish name or rarity...

nerds.subtitle: For Spreadsheet Maintainers and Data Miners. The underlying physics and math of the Fish! engine.
nerds.s1_p1: The game does not use flat random numbers for weight. It rolls a hidden decimal (0.0 to 1.0), applies your Big Catch points as a shift, and plots it on a Sine wave.
nerds.s2_p1: Because the effective roll hard-caps at 1.0, negative points make it mathematically impossible to reach the top of the curve.
nerds.s2_p2:
If you have -90 Big Catch, your maximum possible effective roll is 0.7 (1.0 - 0.3).
Math.sin(0.7 * (π / 2)) = 0.8910.
nerds.s2_p3: Conclusion: At -90 Big Catch, you can never catch a fish larger than 89.10% of its true maximum size.
nerds.s3_p1: Because the effective roll floor-caps at 0.0, positive points make it mathematically impossible to reach the bottom of the curve.
nerds.s3_p2:
If you have +90 Big Catch, your minimum possible effective roll is 0.3 (0.0 + 0.3).
Math.sin(0.3 * (π / 2)) = 0.4539.
nerds.s3_p3: Conclusion: At +90 Big Catch, you can never catch a fish smaller than 45.39% of its potential size range.
nerds.s4_p1: You do not need to remove stats to find a fish's true values. By logging your extreme highs and lows, we can reverse the math for both the Ceiling and the Floor.
nerds.s4_scen1_l1: // SCENARIO A: Solving Max Weight (Using a Negative Penalty)
nerds.s4_scen1_l2: // Ex: Caught a 1.00kg max fish at -90 Penalty (Min is known 0.1kg)
nerds.s4_scen2_l1: // SCENARIO B: Solving Min Weight (Using a Positive Buff)
nerds.s4_scen2_l2: // Ex: Caught a 0.56kg min fish at +90 Buff (Max is known 1.11kg)
nerds.s5_p1: The developer confirmed price scales linearly between baseFloor (at Min Weight) and baseCeil (at Max Weight). By catching a "Tiny" mutation (which forces weight to 0.0kg), we force the price equation to extrapolate backward, revealing the exact coin-value per kilogram.
nerds.s5_scen1_l1: // SCENARIO: Caught a 0.0kg Tiny ($29 value) and a 0.1kg Normal ($31 value)
nerds.s5_scen1_l2: // The fish has a known Base Max Weight of 0.5kg
nerds.s5_scen2_l1: // Solving True Base Floor (Price at known Min Weight: 0.1kg)
nerds.s5_scen2_l3: // Solving True Base Ceil (Price at known Max Weight: 0.5kg)
nerds.s6_p1: Every fishing rod in the game has a built-in Max Weight stat (e.g., the Stick and String caps at 5kg). This acts as a hard ceiling on what you can catch.
nerds.s6_p2: DATA WARNING: If a fish's natural calculated weight exceeds your rod's capacity, your data will be artificially flatlined. Always ensure your rod's Max Weight is significantly higher than the fish you are testing, or your reverse-algebra equations will be solving for the rod, not the fish!
nerds.s7_p1: Mutations fundamentally alter the math engine. They do not use separate base stats.
nerds.s7_li1: Tiny: Forces the physical weight variable to exactly 0.0kg. The price engine then extrapolates backward normally using the established Scaling Rate.
nerds.s7_li2: Huge: Multiplies the fish's baseMinW and baseMaxW by 4x, and multiplies the baseFloor and baseCeil by 2x. The Sine curve roll is then applied to these expanded brackets.
nerds.s8_p1: Luck does not linearly add percentage points to your drops. It creates a global Multiplier that modifies each rarity's BaseWeight using an exponential ScaleFactor.
nerds.s8_p2_desc: We mathematically triangulated the game's exact source exponents by anchoring them to 1,000 Luck developer constraints:
nerds.s8_li2: Abundant (Exponent -1.2): Heaviest negative exponent makes it the "most nerfed" rarity.
nerds.s8_li3: Ultimate (Exponent +1.26): Exact factor required to hit 20x the base spawn rate.
nerds.s8_p3: Because common fish have negative exponents, negative/cursed luck will mathematically buff common spawns while crushing rare spawns!
nerds.s9_p1: To accurately forecast XP and expected catch yields per hour, the engine simulates an average "Cycle Time" consisting of three distinct phases:
nerds.s9_li1: Wait (Attraction): A Piecewise Linear Interpolation (Lerp) from 14.0s (0% Attraction) down to 0.0s (100% Attraction).
nerds.s9_li2: Reel Time: A dynamically weighted average based on the specific reel times of the current Luck Pool.
nerds.s9_li3: Cast Delay: A static 1.5s human/animation penalty applied to every cycle.

credits.accuracy_desc: The Luck Scaling, Big Catch Limits, and Economy Pricing algorithms in this tool have been mathematically triangulated to perfectly match explicit constraints and hints provided directly by the TrickForge Developers.
credits.note_desc: While the core physics engine is exact, community-sourced Database values (like specific fish weights) may still contain slight UI rounding errors.
